museum.dek: June 2009: E3 2009 reveals motion-control futures: Project Natal and PlayStation's motion controller prototype.

sections.title: Releases
sections.subtitle: Eight notable releases, led by month-specific anchors where evidence supports them.

items.title: Wii Sports Resort
items.body: MotionPlus sells a more precise version of the Wii dream.
items.sourceNote: Regional timing varies between June and July 2009.

items.title: Street Fighter IV
items.body: Capcom brings fighting games back to the front of the shop.
items.sourceNote: Console release widely listed for February 2009.

items.title: Demon's Souls
items.body: FromSoftware's bleak RPG begins in Japan before word of mouth travels west.
items.sourceNote: Japan release February 2009; North America October, Europe 2010.

items.title: Resident Evil 5
items.body: Co-op action moves Resident Evil further from horror and closer to spectacle.
items.sourceNote: Widely listed for March 2009.

items.title: Plants vs. Zombies
items.body: PopCap turns lane defence into a desktop comfort object.
items.sourceNote: Widely listed for May 2009.

items.title: Minecraft public development release
items.body: A rough building toy quietly begins one of gaming's largest cultural stories.
items.sourceNote: Minecraft references list May 17, 2009.

items.title: inFamous
items.body: Sucker Punch gives PS3 a comic-book city and a morality meter.
items.sourceNote: Widely listed for May 2009.

items.title: Batman: Arkham Asylum
items.body: Rocksteady makes licensed superhero games respectable overnight.
items.sourceNote: Widely listed for August 2009.

sections.title: Hardware
sections.subtitle: Platform, buying and industry context for the month and its wider year.

items.title: PS3 Slim launches
items.body: Sony's smaller, cheaper PS3 helps the machine feel less forbidding.
items.sourceNote: PlayStation Blog confirms September 2009 slim model and price.

items.title: Motion control's next wave is previewed
items.body: Microsoft's Project Natal and Sony's motion prototype show every platform chasing Wii's audience.
items.sourceNote: E3 2009 context; verify exact stage details before final copy.

items.title: Wii MotionPlus arrives
items.body: Nintendo sells a more precise version of Wii control through Wii Sports Resort.
items.sourceNote: Wii Sports Resort/MotionPlus 2009 context supports this reading.

items.title: Digital PC culture keeps growing
items.body: Steam, mods, indie games and early Minecraft communities make PC feel increasingly account-and-community based.
items.sourceNote: Minecraft/Steam context supports this year-level reading.

sections.title: Online Life
sections.subtitle: How the network felt around the edges of play.

items.title: Services and servers
items.body: Modern Warfare 2 makes party chat, prestige, killstreaks and online identity ordinary language.
items.sourceNote: Online-life context is synthesised from platform history, period reporting and UK access patterns; verify with period web/magazine sources before final publication.

items.title: Friends and parties
items.body: Minecraft starts as a small public build shared through forums and word of mouth.
items.sourceNote: Online-life context is synthesised from platform history, period reporting and UK access patterns; verify with period web/magazine sources before final publication.

items.title: Downloads and stores
items.body: Demon's Souls uses messages, ghosts and invasions to make online play feel mysterious rather than merely social.
items.sourceNote: Online-life context is synthesised from platform history, period reporting and UK access patterns; verify with period web/magazine sources before final publication.

items.title: Everyday connection
items.body: Digital stores, DLC and patches are now part of the purchase, not the aftercare.
items.sourceNote: Online-life context is synthesised from platform history, period reporting and UK access patterns; verify with period web/magazine sources before final publication.

sections.title: What It Felt Like
sections.subtitle: A curator's narrative sketch of the month as lived culture.

items.title: The room
items.body: It felt like online identity had become ordinary. Friends lists, parties, ranks, patches and downloadable extras were no longer surprising.
items.sourceNote: Interpretive narrative based on researched anchors; keep labelled as atmosphere rather than fact.

items.title: The shelf
items.body: Modern Warfare 2 owned the noise, but Minecraft and Demon's Souls were quieter signals from the future.
items.sourceNote: Interpretive narrative based on researched anchors; keep labelled as atmosphere rather than fact.

items.title: The conversation
items.body: PS3 Slim changed the mood around Sony's console: less luxury object, more plausible purchase.
items.sourceNote: Interpretive narrative based on researched anchors; keep labelled as atmosphere rather than fact.

items.title: The afterimage
items.body: The decade ended with three different futures visible at once: blockbuster online shooters, strange connected cult games and worlds players would build themselves.
items.sourceNote: Interpretive narrative based on researched anchors; keep labelled as atmosphere rather than fact.
